extra.message: Direct assignment to innerHTML with potentially untrusted data. If the value originates from API responses or URL parameters, this is a DOM XSS vulnerability. Use textContent or DOMPurify.sanitize().

Token stored in localStorage. localStorage persists indefinitely and is accessible to any XSS. Use sessionStorage (cleared on tab close) or HttpOnly cookies.

Token stored in sessionStorage. Better than localStorage (cleared on tab close) but still accessible to XSS within the same tab. Consider HttpOnly cookies for highest security.
